title: गोपाल सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गोपाल सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ गोपालो गोपतिर्गोपी गोचरो गोमयप्रियः।
गोवर्धनो गोपवरो गोपेशो गोकुलोद्भवः॥ १॥

कृष्णः कमलपत्राक्षः कामदेवः कलानिधिः।
कंसारिः कालियमर्दनः कमलानाथः केशवः॥ २॥

मुरारिर्माधवो मोहनो मदनमोहनः।
मुकुन्दो मधुसूदनो मुरलीधरो मनोहरः॥ ३॥

वासुदेवो विष्णुश्च वनमाली विभूषितः।
वृन्दावनचरो विश्वो वेणुवादनतत्परः॥ ४॥

नन्दनन्दनो नागारिर्नवनीतप्रियो नटः।
नारायणो निर्गुणश्च निरञ्जनो निरामयः॥ ५॥

दामोदरो दयानिधिर्द्वारकानाथ उत्तमः।
देवकीनन्दनो दिव्यो देवदेवो दयापरः॥ ६॥

गोकुलानन्दकारी च गोपीवस्त्रापहारकः।
गोवर्धनगिरिधारी गोपालकृष्ण ईश्वरः॥ ७॥

राधावल्लभ रासेशो रमणीयो रमाप्रियः।
रुक्मिणीरमणो राजा रणवीरो रघूत्तमः॥ ८॥

यशोदानन्दनो योगी यदुवंशप्रदीपकः।
यमुनातीरविहारी यज्ञभोक्ता युगावहः॥ ९॥

बालगोपालो बालकृष्णो बृहद्भानुर्बलप्रदः।
ब्रजवासी ब्रजेशश्च बृन्दावनविहारकृत्॥ १०॥

शौरिः शरणदः श्रेष्ठः शंखचक्रगदाधरः।
श्यामसुन्दरो शान्तश्च शत्रुघ्नो शरणागतः॥ ११॥

हरिर्हृषीकेशश्च हलधरानुजो हयग्रीवः।
हृदयवासी हिरण्यगर्भश्च हव्यवाहनप्रियः॥ १२॥

अनन्तोऽच्युतो अव्ययो अजितोऽमरप्रभुः।
आनन्दमूर्तिरव्यक्तो अधोक्षजो अमृतप्रदः॥ १३॥

पुण्डरीकाक्षः पुरुषोत्तमः परमेश्वरः।
पूतात्मा पद्मनाभश्च प्रद्युम्नो प्रतिदः प्रभुः॥ १४॥

चतुर्भुजश्चक्रपाणिश्चित्तचोरश्चिदात्मकः।
चन्द्रवदनो चारुहासश्चराचरपतिश्च सः॥ १५॥

त्रिविक्रमस्त्रिलोकेशो त्रिगुणातीत ईश्वरः।
तारकस्तुलसीप्रियस्तृप्तिदस्तमोनुदः॥ १६॥

जगन्नाथो जनार्दनो ज्ञानगम्यो जगत्पतिः।
जगत्सृष्टिकरो जीवो ज्योतिरूपो जलेशयः॥ १७॥

विश्वकर्ता विश्वभर्ता विश्वात्मा विश्वतोमुखः।
वैकुण्ठनाथो विजयी विराट्रूपो विमुक्तिदः॥ १८॥

सच्चिदानन्दः सर्वज्ञः सर्वशक्तिमान् प्रभुः।
सनातनः सत्यसंकल्पः सर्वभूतहिते रतः॥ १९॥

एवं सहस्रनामानि गोपालस्य महात्मनः।
यः पठेत् प्रातरुत्थाय सर्वकामानवाप्नुयात्॥ २०॥

॥ इति श्री गोपाल सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Gopala Sahasranama Stotram ||

Om Gopalo Gopatir-Gopi Gocharo Gomaya-priyah |
Govardhano Gopa-varo Gopesho Gokulod-bhavah || 1 ||

Krishnah Kamala-patrakshah Kamadevah Kalanidhih |
Kamsarih Kaliya-mardanah Kamala-nathah Keshavah || 2 ||

Murarir-Madhavo Mohano Madana-Mohanah |
Mukundo Madhusudano Murali-dharo Manoharah || 3 ||

Vasudevo Vishnushcha Vanamali Vibhushitah |
Vrindavana-charo Vishvo Venu-vadana-tatparah || 4 ||

Nanda-nandano Nagarir-Navanita-priyo Natah |
Narayano Nirgunashcha Niranjano Niramayah || 5 ||

Damodaro Daya-nidhir-Dwarakanatha Uttamah |
Devaki-nandano Divyo Deva-devo Dayaparah || 6 ||

Gokulananda-kari cha Gopi-vastra-paharakah |
Govardhana-giri-dhari Gopala-Krishna Ishvarah || 7 ||

Radha-vallabha Rasesho Ramaniyo Rama-priyah |
Rukmini-ramano Raja Rana-viro Raghuttamah || 8 ||

Yashoda-nandano Yogi Yadu-vamsha-pradipakah |
Yamuna-tira-vihari Yajna-bhokta Yugavahah || 9 ||

Bala-Gopalo Bala-Krishno Brihad-bhanur-Bala-pradah |
Braja-vasi Brajeshashcha Brindavana-vihara-krit || 10 ||

Shaurih Sharanadah Shreshthah Shankha-chakra-gadadharah |
Shyama-sundaro Shantashcha Shatrughno Sharanagath || 11 ||

Harir-Hrishikeshashcha Haladhara-anujo Hayagrivah |
Hridaya-vasi Hiranyagarbhashcha Havya-vahana-priyah || 12 ||

Ananto'chyuto Avyayo Ajito'mara-prabhuh |
Ananda-murtir-avyakto Adhokshajo Amrita-pradah || 13 ||

Pundarikakshah Purushottamah Parameshvarah |
Putatma Padmanabhashcha Pradyumno Pratidah Prabhuh || 14 ||

Chatur-bhujash-chakra-panish-chitta-chorash-chidatmakah |
Chandra-vadano Charu-hasash-charachara-patishcha sah || 15 ||

Trivikramas-trilokesho Trigunatita Ishvarah |
Tarakas-tulasi-priyas-triptidas-tamo-nudah || 16 ||

Jagannathao Janardano Jnana-gamyo Jagatpatih |
Jagat-srishti-karo Jivo Jyoti-rupo Jaleshayah || 17 ||

Vishva-karta Vishva-bharta Vishvatma Vishvato-mukhah |
Vaikuntha-natho Vijayi Virat-rupo Vimukti-dah || 18 ||

Sachchidanandah Sarvajnah Sarva-shaktiman Prabhuh |
Sanantanah Satya-sankalpah Sarva-bhuta-hite ratah || 19 ||

Evam sahasra-namani Gopalasya mahatmanah |
Yah pathet pratar-utthaya sarva-kaman-avapnuyat || 20 ||

|| Iti Shri Gopala Sahasranama Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
गोपाल, गोपति, गोपी, गोचर (गायों में विचरण करने वाले), गोमय प्रिय, गोवर्धन, गोपश्रेष्ठ, गोपों के ईश्वर, गोकुल में उत्पन्न॥

कृष्ण, कमलपत्र नेत्र वाले, कामदेव, कलानिधि, कंस के शत्रु, कालिय मर्दन, कमलानाथ, केशव॥

मुरारि, माधव, मोहन, मदनमोहन, मुकुन्द, मधुसूदन, मुरलीधर, मनोहर॥

वासुदेव, विष्णु, वनमाली, विभूषित, वृन्दावन में विचरण करने वाले, विश्वरूप, वेणुवादन में तत्पर॥

नन्दनन्दन, सर्पों के शत्रु, नवनीत (मक्खन) प्रिय, नट (नाटक करने वाले), नारायण, निर्गुण, निरञ्जन, निरामय॥

दामोदर, दयानिधि, द्वारकानाथ, उत्तम, देवकीनन्दन, दिव्य, देवदेव, दयापर॥

गोकुल में आनन्द करने वाले, गोपियों के वस्त्र हरने वाले, गोवर्धन गिरि धारी, गोपालकृष्ण ईश्वर॥

राधावल्लभ, रास के ईश्वर, रमणीय, रमा (लक्ष्मी) प्रिय, रुक्मिणी रमण, राजा, रणवीर॥

यशोदानन्दन, योगी, यदुवंश के प्रदीपक, यमुना तीर पर विहार करने वाले, यज्ञ भोक्ता॥

बालगोपाल, बालकृष्ण, तेजस्वी, बल प्रदान करने वाले, ब्रजवासी, ब्रजेश, वृन्दावन में विहार करने वाले॥

इस प्रकार महात्मा गोपाल के सहस्र नाम हैं। जो प्रातःकाल उठकर इनका पाठ करता है, वह सभी कामनाओं को प्राप्त करता है॥
benefits: Recitation of 1000 names of Lord Gopala Krishna
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of desires when recited daily at dawn
Invokes the blessing of immense spiritual merit and divine grace
Traditionally associated with prosperity, peace, and happiness
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of obstacles and sins
description: Gopala Sahasranama Stotram — गोपाल सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् — is a sacred recitation of 1000 divine names offered to Lord Krishna. This sacred composition is sourced from Brahmanda Purana and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to bring prosperity, peace, and happiness as well as fulfill all desires when recited daily at dawn. Additionally, it is known to remove all obstacles and sins. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Janmashtami, Holi, and Govardhan Puja. At 20 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 30 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Gopala Sahasranama Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Gopala Sahasranama - 1000 names of Lord Gopala Krishna in Sanskrit with transliteration and Hindi meaning for daily devotional recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Gopala Sahasranama and where does it appear?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gopala Sahasranama is a 1000-name Sanskrit stotra praising Lord Krishna in his Gopala (cowherd / Baby Krishna) form. The principal version appears in the Narada Pancharatra — a major Vaishnava Agama scripture — and is narrated by Lord Shiva to Parvati. The text contains 193 verses in simple Sanskrit listing the 1008 names that capture every dimension of Krishna's Vrindavan-leela.

seoFaqs.question: Who narrated the Gopala Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: In the Narada Pancharatra version, Lord Shiva narrates the Gopala Sahasranama to Parvati at her request — making it a Shaiva endorsement of Vaishnava worship and a bridge between the two traditions. The framing dialogue underscores the principle that Shiva is himself the foremost Krishna-bhakta. Other minor recensions are found within the Padma Purana and various Pancharatra Samhitas.

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Gopala Sahasranama promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti promises removal of poverty (daridra-nashan), relief from suffering, protection from famine, prosperity, healing of illnesses, fulfilment of desires (especially progeny — Gopala being the form of Bal Krishna is recited by couples seeking children), and ultimately moksha as Vaikuntha-prapti or Goloka-prapti. It is considered a 'wish-fulfilment' sahasranama (kamya path).

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Gopala Sahasranama be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: Wednesday (Krishna's day in Vaishnava tradition), Ekadashi, Janmashtami, Radhashtami, Gopashtami, the entire month of Kartik, and Mondays of Shravan are the peak occasions. Couples seeking children traditionally undertake a 41-day path. Recitation is done after bath, facing east, with a Krishna-Gopala murti, offering tulsi leaves, butter, milk, and yellow flowers. A complete path takes about 90 minutes.

seoFaqs.question: How does Gopala Sahasranama differ from Vishnu Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vishnu Sahasranama (from the Anushasana Parva of Mahabharata, taught by Bhishma to Yudhishthira) covers Vishnu's universal Para-Brahman aspect across all his avatars and abodes. The Gopala Sahasranama (from the Narada Pancharatra, narrated by Shiva to Parvati) focuses exclusively on Krishna in his Bal-Gopala / Vrindavan form — his pastoral leela, his relationship with Radha, the gopis, cows and Vrindavan. Devotees often recite both as complementary sahasranamas.
